text:
There once was a boy named Melville. He lived in a small village called Willow Creek. One bright morning, Melville heard a soft giggling.

He followed the sound to a thicket, where he discovered a group of woodland sprites, humming happily. Among them was Melly the Merry Mouse, always bouncing on those magical wings.

"You're laughing, aren't you?" Melville asked.

"Always," they chuckled. "Because we've just discovered the most delightful thing!"

"Mystery?"

"You're our new star!" they squeaked.

Melly waved fluffily. Melville was quite surprised. He took a closer look—his reflection! But it was dancing, singing, twinkling... a beautiful, tiny elf.

"Hello!" Melville said. "I am Melville. What do I get?"

"A magical flute!" said a sprite, picking it up.

"And... what?"

"You play the enchanting limericks it sings!"
